rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are valid and visible in the current scope before using them in AGREE contracts.
When AGREE reports type mismatches, ensure that expressions on both sides of assignments or guarantees have matching types.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always reference only identifiers that are declared or visible in the same annex or component scope.
Validate that all helper declarations and constants use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool).
When a guarantee or assumption references a feature or property, ensure the referenced element exists and is properly typed in the AADL model.
Avoid introducing package-qualified names in AGREE annexes unless explicitly required and available in the model's configured dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions and guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings and assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements or references that are not already present in the AADL model.
Do not assume that AADL identifiers are automatically available in AGREE without proper declaration or visibility.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
Component type vs implementation scope rules
